description_wiki: Juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA), also known as juvenile arthritis and formerly known as juvenile rheumatoid arthritis (JRA), is the most common chronic rheumatic disease of childhood, affecting approximately 3.8 to 400 out of 100,000 children.
